title: [Features of facial expressions in patients with cognitive impairment due to Alzheimer's disease].
abstract: OBJECTIVE: To determine the prospect of using the author's method of video analysis of facial expressions as an auxiliary method in diagnosing Alzheimer's disease. MATERIAL AND METHODS: The study included clinically healthy individuals (n=37) and patients (n=34) with neurocognitive disorder (NCD) caused by Alzheimer's disease. The patients were divided into subgroups of subjects with "mild" NCD (n=16) and "major" NCD (n=18). The method is based on a no-contact capture of 68 key points of the face. Kinetic indicators of eyebrow, eyelid, and mouth movements are analyzed during the following diagnostic tests: "Smile", "Drawing the letter O with the lips", "Blinking", "Raising eyebrows", "Frowning eyebrows", and "Reading the test". RESULTS: When comparing the obtained data on the state of facial expressions of healthy individuals with a group of patients, a statistically significant difference (p<0.05) was found in the amplitude and speed of movements of the eyebrows, eyelids, and mouth. Significant differences (p<0.05) in "Smile frequency", "Eyebrow raising frequency", and "Eyebrow raising amplitude along the X axis" indicators were also found between the two subgroups of patients with NCD. In the subgroup of patients with "major" NCD compared to "mild" NCD, there was a decrease in the amplitude of eyebrow raising along the X-axis, the frequency of eyebrow raising by approximately 2 times, and the smile frequency by 1.6 times. CONCLUSION: The method is promising as a screening and additional diagnostic tool in patients with suspected Alzheimer's disease. However, further studies are required to determine its diagnostic value.
venue: Zhurnal nevrologii i psikhiatrii imeni S.S. Korsakova
